name: Application Whitelisting Bypass via DNX Execution
description: Detects suspicious execution of `dnx.exe`, the .NET Execution Environment host. Adversaries can abuse this legitimate Microsoft binary to bypass application whitelisting solutions (e.g., AppLocker) by executing arbitrary code. This technique leverages `dnx.exe` as a signed binary proxy to achieve code execution while evading restrictions on untrusted executables or scripts.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the host **{computer_name}**: the process `dnx.exe` executed the command line **{command_line}**, which contains patterns indicative of **Signed Binary Proxy Execution** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1218**). This technique is a known method for bypassing application whitelisting and executing arbitrary code. The parent process was **{parent_image}** and the user was **{user}**. While `dnx.exe` is a legitimate developer tool, its use to execute arbitrary code from suspicious locations is highly malicious. Immediate investigation is required to determine the nature of the code being executed and whether this is a legitimate developer activity or a malicious attack.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line that includes arguments for dnx.exe.

parameters.name: parent_image
parameters.description: The parent process that launched dnx.exe.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the process ran.

parameters.name: computer_name
parameters.description: The name of the computer where the event occurred.
